title: Heterotheca ruthii
intro: Heterotheca ruthii, also known as Ruth's fleabane, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Heterotheca ruthii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Heterotheca ruthii is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it in the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Asteraceae is further divided into several subfamilies, and Heterotheca ruthii is specifically placed within the subfamily Asteroideae. The genus Heterotheca is a group of flowering plants native to North America, and Heterotheca ruthii is one of its species. The classification of Heterotheca ruthii reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
